title: Sei settimane per spegnere la guerra. Biden invita Hamas ad accettare l'accordo
en_title: Six weeks to turn off the war. Biden invites Hamas to accept the agreement
en_content:
AGI - Hamas is warmly invited by the President of the United States himself, Joe Biden, to accept a new proposal for agreement > in three stages put on the table by Israel, because "it is time for this war to end". The head of the White House announced it by surprise, in a speech in which he outlined a possible solution to the conflict, in course for almost 8 months. The proposal, explained the same president, previews a first phase of six weeks in which the Israeli armed forces would withdraw from all the inhabited areas of the Gaza Strip >, and hoped that it will start "after tomorrow". "It is a decisive moment, we cannot lose it - said Biden - After an intense diplomatic activity, carried out by my team, in conversations with the leaders of Israel, Qatar, Egypt and other countries of the Middle East, Israel offered a new global proposal for a ceasefire and the release of all hostages," he said. In his speech, Biden insisted that the proposal, already sent to Hamas through Qatar, is also favourable for Hamas. In the first phase, he said, there would be a complete ceasefire for 6 weeks, during which Israeli troops would retreat from all the populated areas of the Strip and several hostages would be released, including women, elderly and wounded, in exchange for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners. During these six weeks, Israel and Hamas should negotiate the details of the second phase, which would imply "the final end of hostility", the release of the rest of the hostages, including soldiers, and the withdrawal of the Israeli army from the Strip. According to Biden, this second phase will require very intense negotiations, and the ceasefire should not be violated > until they continue. The third and final phase would provide a "great reconstruction plan" for the Palestinian enclave and the return of the bodies of the murdered hostages. "If Hamas maintains its commitments, the ceasefire will become, according to the text of the Israeli proposal, a permanent cessation of hostilities," Biden added. "This is the offer that is now on the table. That is what we ask and what we need," said Biden. Hamas has on several occasions reiterated that for any agreement the permanent truce and complete Israeli retreat "are not negotiable". Biden did not mention the situation in Rafah, but he defined "a hell" > how much the Palestinian population is endured, adding that it only has to gain from the acceptance of the proposal.   

Hamas: "Positive Biden's speech"

Hamas claims to "consider positively" the Israeli roadmap towards a ceasefire announced by US President Joe Biden after almost eight months of war in Gaza.
"Hamas considers positive" the contents of Biden's speech today about "a ceasefire, the withdrawal of Israeli forces from Gaza, reconstruction and exchange of prisoners", said the Palestinian Islamic movement in a statement.
